sections.title: The 3 R's and ESP Method
items: Read — Read the problem thoroughly.
Represent — Represent the unknown through a variable.
Relate — Relate variables to each other and to given values.
Equate — Write an equation using facts in the problem.
Solve — Solve the equation.
Prove — Check the answer.
Note: The value of x is not always the final answer — substitute x back into the representation.

sections.title: Translating English to Algebra
rows: plus; more than; increased by; sum of
minus; less than; subtracted from; decreased by; difference
4 less than twice the number is 10
3 times the sum of the number and 4

sections.title: Consecutive Integers
items: Consecutive integers: x, x+1, x+2, x+3, …
Consecutive odd/even integers: x, x+2, x+4, x+6, … (gap is always 2)

sections.title: Age Problems
items: Ages of all characters change at the same rate.
Assign variables to represent ages in the present.
Use a table to organize ages across different time frames.
2 years ago → age − 2
Now → age
5 years from now → age + 5

sections.title: Work Problems
items: Formula: 1/x + 1/y = 1/z
x = time for person 1 to complete the job alone
y = time for person 2 to complete it alone
z = time for both working together
In 1 hour: person x does 1/x of the job; person y does 1/y; together they do 1/z.

sections.title: Motion Problems
items: Formula: distance = speed × time
Use a table with columns: Distance, Speed, Time.
Moving toward each other: add the distances. d₁ + d₂ = total distance
Moving in opposite directions: same as above.
One chasing the other: set distances equal.

sections.title: Problem Solving Strategies
items: Working Backwards: Used when a series of events leads to a known final value. Reverse all operations: addition ↔ subtraction; multiplication ↔ division.
Make an Organized List: Used for trial-and-error problems. List all possible values based on the first description, then eliminate based on the next descriptions.
Making a Diagram: Useful for visualizing motion or distance problems. Draw a line; mark distances per unit of time.
